subject: Project Management
title: Y2K: The Biggest Fix
description: A micro-lesson that makes Y2K: The Biggest Fix usable.

hook.text: Old computers stored years with two digits. When 2000 arrived, they'd think it was 1900.

buildup.text: Banks, airlines, power grids, and military systems all ran on code written decades earlier.

discovery.text: Governments spent over $300 billion to mobilize millions of programmers for the fix.

twist.text: January 1, 2000 arrived with barely a glitch. Critics called it an overreaction.

climax.text: It wasn't overreaction — it was one of the most successful global PM efforts ever.

punchline.text: The best crisis response is one where nothing happens.

quiz.question: Why was the Y2K bug a problem?
options: Computers stored years with two digits and couldn't handle 2000
The internet was about to collapse
All computers needed to be replaced
